summary.intent: Add bundled default skills to Moltis by copying vetted skills from Hermes Agent and OpenClaw, embedding them in the binary, and surfacing them in the web UI with category filtering.
summary.outcome: 101 bundled skills (69 from Hermes, 32 from OpenClaw) embedded via include_dir!, with category metadata, origin tracking, requires: frontmatter, Hermes reference cleanup, a composite discoverer with priority ordering, web UI category filter pills, 15 automated tests, and a char-budget format fallback for system prompt generation.

repo: Skills use a SKILL.md frontmatter format with fields: name, description, origin, requires (bins/any_bins/install), platforms. Moltis-native fields only — no metadata/dependencies/version/author from Hermes.
Skill discovery has priority order: Project > Personal > Registry > Plugin > Bundled. User-created skills override bundled by name.
Skills crate feature-gates bundled skills behind 'bundled-skills' feature (default-on in CLI and gateway).
CompositeSkillDiscoverer merges FS + bundled sources with name deduplication.
Dev mode reads bundled skills from filesystem for instant iteration; release mode serves from include_dir! embedded binary.
Hermes and OpenClaw both use name+description index in system prompt (~1500-2500 tokens for 70-100 skills), then on-demand full content via a read_skill tool — same pattern Moltis already uses.
OpenClaw adds a char budget with compact fallback (drop descriptions, keep names) when over limit.
Directory names must match SKILL.md frontmatter 'name' field — mismatch causes read_skill() to silently fail.

code.finding: BundledSkillStore wraps include_dir! with dev-mode filesystem fallback; discovery is recursive to handle mlops sub-categories (3+ levels deep).
Added CompositeSkillDiscoverer that composes FsSkillDiscoverer + BundledSkillStore with deduplication. watcher.rs had an exhaustive match on SkillSource that required a Bundled no-op arm.
generate_skills_prompt now accepts max_chars budget with compact fallback: drops descriptions and keeps only names when over budget.
SkillMetadata gained: SkillSource::Bundled variant, SkillOrigin struct (source, url, commit, copied_at), category field derived from asset directory path.
ReadSkillTool gains with_bundled() constructor and read_bundled() function that serves content from memory for bundled skills instead of hitting the filesystem.
Web UI skill listing uses CompositeSkillDiscoverer. Had a lint error: Arc was already imported but new code used std::sync::Arc:: fully-qualified path.
Added category filter pills above enabled skills table and category badges per row. Category field added to Skill type.

workflow: Python script injection of YAML frontmatter is fragile with sed — sed truncated files; a Python script with proper frontmatter parsing was needed.

When copying external skill repos, run a name/directory mismatch check before assuming discoverability — 6 skills had mismatches (e.g., directory 'trl-fine-tuning' vs frontmatter name 'fine-tuning-with-trl').
Tests are valuable during bulk copy operations: the bundled skill tests immediately caught the 6 directory/name mismatches that would have silently failed at runtime.
Node modules aren't installed in worktrees by default — need 'npm install' before TypeScript type-checking or Vite builds.
Disk space can be exhausted running full cargo test in worktrees with large dependencies (llama-cpp); use cargo check + targeted test runs instead.

friction: sed-based YAML frontmatter injection truncated skill files; required re-copying from Hermes and switching to a Python script.
Two exhaustive matches on SkillSource (in discover.rs and watcher.rs) caused compile errors after adding the Bundled variant.
Arc lint error in services.rs: new code used fully-qualified std::sync::Arc::new() when Arc was already imported in scope.
Background bash tasks hung silently when comparing openclaw vs bundled skills — required switching to direct execution.
Node modules not present in worktree required manual npm install before frontend work could proceed.
Disk exhaustion prevented running full cargo test suite in worktree (llama-cpp compilation fills disk).
The initial audit of Hermes skill bodies for dependencies was too shallow and missed most skills, requiring a second pass.
open_items: Some skills still reference '~/.hermes/' paths or 'Hermes Integration' sections that weren't caught by the bulk cleanup regex — a follow-up manual review pass was noted but not completed.
OpenClaw-specific skills (clawflow, clawhub, node-connect, healthcheck, canvas, voice-call, session-logs, model-usage) were explicitly excluded — could revisit if Moltis adds equivalent capabilities.
Extension skills from OpenClaw (Feishu, QQBot, ACP) were excluded as platform-specific — could be added if Moltis gains those integrations.
Char budget compact fallback added to prompt_gen but no configuration surface exists yet — budget is hardcoded, could be exposed as a config option.

Large sidecar reference files (unsloth/references/llms-full.md at 16.8k lines, powerpoint XSD schemas ~15k lines) significantly inflate binary size — noted but left in as the source repo was vouched for.
Category field is only extracted from directory path for bundled skills; user-created FS skills don't yet have category support.
